payload.description: Record gate BLOCK decisions as durable local telemetry so the report can show prevention-success (Stage 2)

payload.reason: automated review round frozen for caller-owned execution

checklist.note: The plan does not preserve the required distinction between in-anger prevention effects and retry/self-correction noise.
The lifecycle ordering is unexecutable under the gate, and the proposed deduplication cannot support the rendered metric claim.
The artifact lacks required change identity and omits files that its tasks explicitly modify.

findings.summary: Task 7 defers `plan ready`, plan review, and `implementation start` until after Tasks 1–6 have already edited, tested, and committed the implementation (`code already TDD'd`). The repository gate blocks those edits before IMPLEMENTATION_IN_PROGRESS, so the plan cannot be executed as written without bypassing the lifecycle it is supposed to dogfood. Move lifecycle setup and approval before the first implementation task.

Deduplicating solely by `(change_id, file, state)` cannot distinguish an agent retry from multiple genuinely separate blocked edits to the same file while the change remains in one state. It therefore undercounts real blocks while the report labels the result as N edits kept from landing, and it does not resolve the binding Stage-2 question of separating in-anger effects from self-corrected noise. Define a defensible attempt identity or render the value explicitly as distinct blocked file/state tuples.

The plan artifact lacks the required YAML `change: record-gate-blocks` identity, and its declared scope omits files the tasks explicitly touch: existing `tests/unit/core/test_paths.py`, `private/OPEN-ITEMS.md`, the ledger, and `private/NEXT-SESSION-PROMPT.md`. This will create scope drift and prevents the artifact from carrying the change identity required by the repository conventions.

checklist.note: All three goals covered by Tasks 4-6; footnote retired.

Fail-open preserved (mirrors verified _record_bypass); telemetry/event separation correct; no hot-path regression; no import cycle; edits_blocked no-default ordering legal; dedup undercounts (honest).
Scope (files) block omits tests/unit/core/test_paths.py (exists, Task 1 edits) and private/OPEN-ITEMS.md (named in Task 7 scope cmd).

findings.summary: Scope (files) block omits tests/unit/core/test_paths.py, but that file exists and Task 1 edits it; self-host merge gate requires plan-ready scope to cover every changed file.
Internal inconsistency: Task 7's plan-ready --scope lists private/OPEN-ITEMS.md but the Scope (files) block omits it.

checklist.note: claude: Fail-open preserved (mirrors verified _record_bypass); telemetry/event separation correct; no hot-path regression; no import cycle; edits_blocked no-default ordering legal; dedup undercounts (honest).; codex: The lifecycle ordering is unexecutable under the gate, and the proposed deduplication cannot support the rendered metric claim.

claude: Scope (files) block omits tests/unit/core/test_paths.py (exists, Task 1 edits) and private/OPEN-ITEMS.md (named in Task 7 scope cmd).; codex: The artifact lacks required change identity and omits files that its tasks explicitly modify.
claude: All three goals covered by Tasks 4-6; footnote retired.; codex: The plan does not preserve the required distinction between in-anger prevention effects and retry/self-correction noise.

payload.reason: automated review round frozen for caller-owned execution

checklist.note: The plan does not update or test the --brief report path, which would continue to omit gate-held targets and can report "caught 0" despite recorded prevention evidence.
Phase 0 explicitly instructs writing through the shell to evade the live pre-tool-use gate, contrary to the repository's no-workaround rule.
The verification phase conditionally directs an unspecified lifecycle spec edit that is absent from the exact declared scope.

findings.summary: Phase 0 tells the implementer to author the plan through the shell specifically while normal edit tools are gated. That is a deliberate workaround of the pre-tool-use gate and conflicts with AGENTS.md's instruction to stop and surface a gate block instead of bypassing it. The lifecycle must provide an authorized plan-authoring path or order plan drafting before the blocking transition.

Task 6 covers the full human renderer, bottom line, and JSON but omits `_render_brief`. The existing brief renderer will still output `caught 0` and no held-target count when `edits_blocked > 0`, undermining the new prevention signal in a supported report mode. Add brief rendering semantics and a regression test.

The plan says to update the lifecycle spec if verification detects canonical-path drift, but no lifecycle spec is named in the exact scope at lines 15-26. That instruction would require an out-of-scope edit. Name and declare the affected spec now, or require an explicit scope redeclaration before making the conditional edit.

checklist.note: All three goals covered by Tasks 1-6; footnote retirement matches current cli/report.py.

Fail-open double-guarded, record before return block, headline test present; no hot-path regression; honest distinct-target floor; snapshot fields verified; edits_blocked ordering safe; agent-agnostic at _decide.
Every touched file listed and exists; internally consistent; no bogus change: frontmatter.

claude: Fail-open double-guarded, record before return block, headline test present; no hot-path regression; honest distinct-target floor; snapshot fields verified; edits_blocked ordering safe; agent-agnostic at _decide.; codex: Phase 0 explicitly instructs writing through the shell to evade the live pre-tool-use gate, contrary to the repository's no-workaround rule.

claude: Every touched file listed and exists; internally consistent; no bogus change: frontmatter.; codex: The verification phase conditionally directs an unspecified lifecycle spec edit that is absent from the exact declared scope.

payload.reason: automated review round frozen for caller-owned execution

checklist.note: Brief-mode coverage was added, but its assertion does not enforce the plan's honest distinct-target terminology.
The proposed brief output loses the metric's unit and can be read as a raw blocked-edit count.
The plan scope matches the latest declared plan-ready scope, and conditional drift now requires redeclaration.

findings.summary: The new brief-mode design renders only `{edits_blocked} blocked`, which omits the distinct-target unit and reads like a raw count of blocked edits or attempts. That contradicts the plan's central requirement to render the conservative `(change_id, file, state)` floor as held targets across all modes. Render an explicit phrase such as `2 distinct targets held` and make the regression test assert that terminology.

checklist.note: Records blocks + surfaces in all report modes (human/brief/json/bottom_line/footnote); Stage-1 footnote retired. No gap.

Fail-open preserved; telemetry outside events.jsonl; honest distinct-target floor; edits_blocked Band-1 ordering safe; CODX-003 ordering sound (draft before change start, no prescribed bypass); CODX-005 verified no spec edit needed.
Every task-touched file listed; post-merge gitignored artifacts correctly excluded; internally consistent.

payload.reason: automated review round frozen for caller-owned execution

checklist.note: Implementation matches the approved out-of-band telemetry and conservative distinct-target metric.
The frozen delta stays within the assigned inspection paths.
BLOCK decisions remain fail-safe when telemetry recording fails; parsing and aggregation are tolerant.

Static review covered missing/malformed logs, timestamp windows, retries, file-less records, and all render modes. Focused pytest could not start because the read-only environment provides no writable temporary directory.
Internal and generated CLI-facing descriptions still characterize the report as event-only.

findings.summary: The module description and --since/--until help still say the report reads or counts only events, but this change also reads and filters gate-block telemetry; update the source help so generated CLI documentation accurately describes all report signals.

checklist.note: Code matches approved plan; all four hard constraints verified against real code.
Only declared files changed; telemetry out of events.jsonl, not wired into reducer/attestation/merge-gate.
Double-layer try/except defense-in-depth; lazy import confined to BLOCK branch; window filter mirrors _in_window.
Missing file/dir, unwritable path, malformed lines, file=null, unparseable ts, retry dedup, no-active-change skip — all handled and tested.
Plan doc added; footnote retired; gitignore comment+path+mirror updated; no spec enumerates canonical paths.

claude: Double-layer try/except defense-in-depth; lazy import confined to BLOCK branch; window filter mirrors _in_window.; codex: BLOCK decisions remain fail-safe when telemetry recording fails; parsing and aggregation are tolerant.
